name: Terminal
description: A real, interactive shell in a pane — a live PTY (bash/PowerShell/cmd) you can type into, run TUI apps like vim or htop, resize, and restart. Distinct from Bash Stream (which passively mirrors the agent's commands): this is YOUR own terminal, spawned in the conversation's working directory. One shell per conversation, persistent while the plugin is enabled. The agent shares this exact shell: terminal_send types a command (or raw keystrokes), terminal_read returns the current ANSI-stripped scrollback, and terminal_interrupt sends Ctrl-C — so the agent can start and tail a long-running monitor (e.g. `vmstat -w 1`) in the same pane the user is watching.

tools.name: terminal_send
tools.description: Type into the shared Terminal pane's live shell, exactly as if the user typed it. By default a carriage return is appended so the line runs as a command; pass enter:false to send raw keystrokes without submitting (e.g. to answer an interactive prompt or send an escape sequence). Output appears in the user's pane; read it back with terminal_read. Does NOT return command output — the shell is asynchronous, so send, then read after the command has had time to produce output.

tools.name: terminal_read
tools.description: Return the current scrollback of the shared Terminal pane's shell (ANSI escape sequences stripped, carriage returns normalized to newlines). Use tailLines to get only the last N lines — ideal for polling a running monitor like `vmstat -w 1`. Pass raw:true to get the untouched byte stream including escape codes.

tools.name: terminal_interrupt
tools.description: Send Ctrl-C (SIGINT, 0x03) to the shared Terminal pane's shell — stops a running foreground process such as a monitoring loop started with terminal_send.
